exercises.sentence: Ele nasceu ___ a guerra acabou.
exercises.explanation: 'Quando' (when) places two events at the same point in time. 'Então' would wrongly imply his birth caused the war to end.

exercises.sentence: Quero ir à praia, ___ preciso trabalhar.
exercises.explanation: 'Mas' (but) expresses that the second idea opposes the first. Wanting the beach conflicts with needing to work. 'Então' would wrongly imply the desire for the beach caused the work obligation.

exercises.sentence: Estava com fome, ___ pedi uma pizza.
exercises.explanation: 'Então' (so/therefore) marks the natural result of being hungry. 'Mas' would imply ordering pizza was a surprising contrast to being hungry — which makes no sense.

exercises.sentence: ___ que você está aqui, pode me ajudar?
exercises.explanation: 'Já que' is a fixed two-word expression meaning 'since' or 'given that'. None of the other options form a valid connector with 'que' in this sentence position.

exercises.sentence: ___ você está triste?
exercises.explanation: 'Por que' (two words) asks 'why' in a direct question. 'Porque' (one word) means 'because' — it answers a question, it does not open one.

exercises.sentence: Ela terminou cedo, ___ pôde descansar.
exercises.explanation: 'Então' (so/therefore) marks the natural result of finishing early. 'Mas' would imply resting was unexpected — which it isn't when you finish early.

exercises.sentence: Eu como arroz ___ feijão todos os dias.
exercises.explanation: 'E' (and) joins two nouns in a list. 'Mas' needs a full contrasting clause after it — it cannot link two bare nouns.

exercises.sentence: Fui ao médico ___ estava com muita febre.
exercises.explanation: 'Porque' (because) gives the reason for the visit. 'Então' would invert the logic, implying the medical visit caused the fever.

exercises.sentence: É importante ___ você estude todos os dias.
exercises.explanation: 'Que' introduces a subjunctive complement clause after impersonal expressions like 'é importante'. 'Se' would create a conditional clause, not the required complement.

exercises.sentence: ___ eu fosse rico, compraria uma casa na praia.
exercises.explanation: 'Se' (if) introduces a counterfactual condition. It pairs with the past subjunctive 'fosse'. 'Quando' (when) requires the future subjunctive 'for', not 'fosse' — making 'quando eu fosse' ungrammatical in this pattern.

exercises.sentence: Fico nervoso ___ tenho que falar em público.
exercises.explanation: 'Quando' (when) links a recurring condition to a habitual emotional response. 'Então' would imply the nervousness causes the speaking obligation — the wrong direction.

exercises.sentence: Não sei ___ o voo vai atrasar.
exercises.explanation: 'Se' (if/whether) introduces an indirect yes/no question after 'saber'. 'Que' introduces a declarative complement — 'não sei que o voo vai atrasar' is not standard Portuguese in this construction.

exercises.sentence: Ele queria dormir, ___ o bebê começou a chorar.
exercises.explanation: 'Mas' (but) signals contrast or an unexpected turn. The crying baby opposes the desire to sleep. 'Então' would wrongly imply that wanting to sleep caused the baby to cry.

exercises.sentence: Ela não veio à festa ___ estava doente.
exercises.explanation: 'Porque' (because) introduces the reason for not attending. 'Mas' would imply she didn't come despite being sick — the opposite of the expected logic.

exercises.sentence: Esse produto é azul ___ verde?
exercises.explanation: 'Ou' (or) presents two alternatives. 'Mas', 'porque', and 'então' require verb clauses — they cannot link two adjectives as choices in a question.

exercises.sentence: Mesmo depois de dois anos, ela ___ sente saudade do Brasil.
exercises.explanation: 'Ainda' (still) expresses that a feeling persists across time. The phrase 'mesmo depois de dois anos' signals duration — 'já' (already) contradicts that framing by implying the feeling is new.

exercises.sentence: Podemos ir de táxi ___ de Uber.
exercises.explanation: 'Ou' (or) offers two travel alternatives. 'Mas', 'porque', and 'então' all require full verb clauses — they cannot link two prepositional phrases as options.

exercises.sentence: O mercado vende frutas, legumes ___ carnes.
exercises.explanation: 'E' (and) joins items in a list. 'Mas', 'então', and 'porque' all require verb clauses after them — they cannot link bare nouns in an enumeration.

exercises.sentence: Eu falo inglês e ___ falo espanhol.
exercises.explanation: 'Também' (also/too) adds a second fact of the same kind. 'Ainda' and 'já' would inject unneeded temporal meaning; 'mas' contradicts the 'e' already in the sentence.

exercises.sentence: Eu acho ___ você tem razão.
exercises.explanation: 'Que' (that) introduces a complement clause after a verb of opinion like 'achar'. 'Se' would turn it into an indirect yes/no question, changing the meaning.
